title: Noodle Flow
description: Schema for a Noodle flow definition (schema_version 1.0). A flow is a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of nodes that together implement a single MCP tool.

schema_version.description: Version of the Noodle flow schema.

name.description: Unique name of the flow within its MCP project. Becomes the MCP tool name.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this flow/tool does. Shown to the agent as the tool description.

input_schema.description: Declares the input parameters accepted by this flow.

nodes.description: List of nodes in the flow DAG.

edges.description: Directed edges that define execution order between nodes.

output.description: Template expression that resolves to the flow's final output. E.g. '{{nodes.format.outputs.result}}'. If omitted, all node outputs are returned.

inputField.description: Declaration of a single input parameter.

description.description: Description shown to the agent for this parameter.

node.description: A single processing unit in the flow.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the flow. Used in template references: {{nodes.<id>.outputs.<field>}}.

type.description: Node type. Determines which built-in executor runs this node.

config.description: Node configuration. Values may include template expressions like {{input.field}} or {{nodes.id.outputs.field}}.

outputs.description: Declares the outputs this node produces (used for documentation and validation).

outputField.description: Declaration of a single output field from a node.

type.description: JSON type of the output value.

edge.description: A directed dependency from one node to another.

to.description: ID of the target node. The target executes after the source.

examples.name: find_customer
examples.description: Finds a customer by email and returns their name and plan.
